pattern: The minimum trips to deliver a prefix of boxes is a DP where each transition's cost only depends on port changes and a capacity window, so folding that window into a monotonic-minimum structure turns an O(n^2) DP into a linear scan.
hints: Think of dp[i] as the fewest trips to deliver the first i boxes; a trip loads a contiguous run of boxes bounded by the ship's box-count and weight limits.
The cost of loading boxes[j..i-1] in one trip is 2 (one trip out, one trip back) plus the number of times consecutive boxes in that run go to different ports.

Because the leftmost valid start for a run only moves forward as the run's right end grows, maintain a sliding window of candidate start points and track the best (dp value minus its cumulative port-change count) with a monotonic structure so each new dp[i] is O(1) amortized.

edge_inputs.description: all boxes fit in one trip but ports differ, forcing extra port visits
capacity forces splitting into multiple loads
maxBoxes = 1, every box needs its own trip
weight limit forces a smaller group than box count would otherwise allow
consecutive boxes to the same port should not add an extra trip
many small port switches back and forth

stress.description: large random box list with random ports/capacity/weight limits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    ports_count = rng.randint(1, 100)
    max_boxes = rng.randint(1, min(n, 20))
    max_weight = rng.randint(max_boxes, max_boxes * 10)
    boxes = [[rng.randint(1, ports_count), rng.randint(1, max_weight)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [boxes, ports_count, max_boxes, max_weight]
